behaviorsAudited: createBridgeTool(<any unrouted name>) returns a tool whose renderToolResultMessage produces the generic <Box><Text>{joined}</Text></Box> output (no routing to BashOutputView or EditDiffView)
Generic fallback handles string content, object content, and array content via the resultTextForBridgeTool helper
Generic fallback never throws on null or undefined content
The dispatch table is name-keyed exact-case; unrouted names do NOT accidentally match a registered renderer (no prefix or substring match across tool names)
Bash and Edit envelope tags do not cross-route — Bash tool name with <edit-diff> content stays generic, Edit tool name with <bash-stdout> stays generic

edgeCasesAudited: createBridgeTool('XYZUnknown') receives a structured-content array (from formatStructuredToolResult) and renders by joining the .text fields — does not throw on the unexpected shape
createBridgeTool('') (empty string name) does not throw and renders the generic fallback
createBridgeTool('bash') (lowercase) does NOT route to BashOutputView — name dispatch is exact-case
createBridgeTool('edit') (lowercase) does NOT route to EditDiffView — name dispatch is exact-case
renderToolResultMessage receives null content and renders without throwing
Unrouted tool name with content that contains a <bash-stdout> envelope does NOT route to BashOutputView (envelope-only matching does not bypass the tool-name gate)
Bash tool name with <edit-diff> envelope (cross-routing attempt) stays generic — envelope tag must match tool name

issues.message: Edge case 'createBridgeTool("XYZUnknown") receives a structured-content array' is decomposed across two assertions rather than asserted as one end-to-end case: resultTextForBridgeTool array-flattening is verified at runtime/src/tools/upstream-tool-result-dispatch.test.ts:96-104 with anonymous block input, and createBridgeTool('XYZUnknown') -> renderToolResultMessage -> generic-fallback is verified with a plain string at runtime/src/tools/tool-stubs-bash-bridge.test.tsx:69-77. The compound case (unknown bridge tool + structured-content array shape, asserting no throw and generic render type) is not asserted in a single test. Behavior is covered by transitive composition; a direct compound assertion would close this gap. Non-blocking.

Edge case 'createBridgeTool("") (empty string name) does not throw and renders the generic fallback' is asserted only at the dispatch-helper level (runtime/src/tools/upstream-tool-result-dispatch.test.ts:51-54 covers pickToolResultDispatch("", ...) -> generic). There is no end-to-end assertion that createBridgeTool("") + renderToolResultMessage() does not throw and returns a generic element. Underlying logic is sound (createBridgeTool accepts any string; dispatch returns generic for any non-Bash/Edit name), so the behavior holds, but a direct end-to-end empty-name assertion would harden the row. Non-blocking.

testsExecuted: node_modules/.bin/vitest run --root . src/tools/upstream-tool-result-dispatch.test.ts src/tools/tool-stubs-bash-bridge.test.tsx src/tools/tool-stubs-edit-bridge.test.tsx src/tools/tool-stubs-extra-bridge.test.tsx (4 test files, 94 tests passed)
